hypothesis: If an LLM-based agent framework that iteratively integrates domain-specific external tools (periodic table, knowledge base, and memory modules) with generative and evaluative models is used for inorganic materials design, then it will achieve higher success rates in targeting specific formation energies and produce more valid, unique, and novel compositions compared to tool-free LLM variants and purely diffusion-based generative approaches, because the structured retrieval and reasoning loop enables the agent to progressively refine compositional proposals using accumulated chemical knowledge and property feedback.
